content.title: Gaza's Cries Drowned by Goal Celebrations
content.subtitle: While the world celebrates, Gaza is fighting for survival.
content.body:
Did you know? Every day, the final cries of innocent children in Gaza are drowned out by the FIFA World Cup excitement of more than 3.5 billion people around the world.

Throughout June and July 2026, while the world has been immersed in the excitement of the FIFA World Cup, a brutal genocide has continued in Gaza, Palestine. On July 8, Reuters reported that 9 Palestinians were killed in Israeli attacks, including 2 children. On the same day, Al Jazeera reported that 8 more people were killed in a separate airstrike, including 2 children. The very next day, July 9, Al Jazeera confirmed that another 10 people had been killed in the previous 24 hours. While we sit comfortably in our living rooms, absorbed in the excitement of the World Cup, lives are being buried beneath the rubble of bombs on the other side of the screen.

Psychic Numbing and Human Desensitization

In psychology, this global cruelty is known as "Psychic Numbing." When people are exposed to prolonged atrocities while simultaneously immersed in intense entertainment, the human mind gradually loses its sensitivity to the suffering of others. As a result, the deaths of countless people on the other side of the screen no longer affect the world as deeply as the victory or defeat of a favorite football team.

Bloodstains Hidden Behind the Glow of Screens

According to digital traffic analysis, nearly 70% of social media attention is now consumed by the overwhelming hype surrounding the World Cup. Once again, this proves that while technology is connecting us more than ever, it is also making us increasingly desensitized and disconnected from humanity.

It Is Our Responsibility to Break This Silence

Take a moment to reflect. Do not let the artificial excitement of the World Cup turn you into someone who has lost their conscience. Raise your voice for the oppressed and persecuted people of Palestine and around the world. Make your child understand that we aren't meant to be enslaved by this kind of cheap entertainment.
